page: Microsoft Azure E32d_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI. The server is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, and includes 1229 GB of local storage. No GPUs are bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the E32d_v4 demonstrates average performance across most compute and memory metrics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1427.99, and the multi-core score is 29830.81. Its memory subsystem achieves a strong PassMark memory score of 2812.46, placing it in the top 25% tier, with read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching 1662.77 GB/sec. Large block uncached read and write bandwidths are measured at 105.21 GB/sec and 47.38 GB/sec, respectively. For database operations, the server processes 27.24 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 11238.96 in PassMark database operations.

The hardware profile presents specific performance tradeoffs. While the instance handles smaller language model workloads adequately, processing 1120.49 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M parameter model, it exhibits poor performance on larger models. Prompt processing for a 70B parameter model drops to 4.51 tokens per second, and text generation data is unavailable, indicating a limitation for large-scale AI inference. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage capacity, the E32d_v4 is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, software compilation, and web serving, rather than large-scale deep learning tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E32d_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. Utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz, alongside 256.0 GB of RAM and 1229 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show average compute capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 29830.81 and a strong PassMark memory score of 2812.46. The server delivers 27.24 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 21.47 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. However, it exhibits poor performance on large-scale LLM inference, with prompt processing for a 70B model limited to 4.51 tokens per second. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32d_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32d_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage on Hyper-V.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-capacity local storage, and strong memory bandwidth for database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8272CL processors
256 GB of system memory paired with 1229 GB of local storage
Strong memory subsystem performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Average database performance with 27.24 million Redis SET operations per second
Suited for memory-intensive databases, web serving, and software compilation
Limited suitability for large-scale LLM inference tasks
